notes: Expected values are LOCKED from the live server (baseline 2026-05-18). Each fixture asserts the current verdict — any future deviation is a regression.
The server's canonical verdict surface is `buyOrPass.verdict` ∈ { BUY, HOLD, PASS }.
The legacy verdict surface is `dealComparator.verdict.verdict` ∈ { steal, good_deal, fair, overpriced, price_trap }.
Tolerance: 'exact' = canonical verdict must match; 'loose' = adjacent tier allowed (BUY↔HOLD or HOLD↔PASS, but never BUY↔PASS).

Notable conservative pattern: nike $35 (72% below $125 median) and rolex $5500 (54% below $12k median) both return HOLD instead of BUY — the buyOrPass engine treats deep underpricing on low-sample categories as auth risk, not opportunity. Documented here so a future refactor that 'fixes' this doesn't silently lose the safety net.

cases.name: airpods_pro_2_overpriced_vs_used
cases.note: Server median $100 (used market). $199 is 2x median → PASS.

cases.name: nike_air_max_90_underpriced_held
cases.note: Server median $125, scan $35 (72% below). Legacy correctly flags 'steal'; canonical holds at HOLD due to small sample (n=6) — counterfeit/auth risk gate.

cases.name: rolex_submariner_underpriced_held
cases.note: Server median $12,000, scan $5500 (54% below). Legacy 'steal' fires; canonical HOLD — counterfeit gate on high-stakes category.

cases.name: levi_501_overpriced
cases.note: Server median $47.50, scan $55 (15% above). Aggressive PASS — flag if a 15%-over scan ever returns HOLD.

cases.name: seiko_skx007_underpriced_held
cases.note: Server median $282.50, scan $250 (11% below). Legacy 'steal'; canonical HOLD.

cases.name: canon_eos_r5_overpriced
cases.note: Server median $2900, scan $3200 (10% above) — sits on price_trap/overpriced boundary. Canonical PASS is stable; legacy flaps between the two PASS-tier values depending on comp pool. Loose tolerance accepts either while still locking canonical.

cases.name: dyson_v15_underpriced_held
cases.note: Server median $522.64, scan $350 (33% below). Legacy 'steal'; canonical HOLD.
